True! I had been very nervous, constantly feeling fear. However, why do you say that I am insane? The illness had made my senses sharper; however, it did not break or dull them in any way. Above everything else, there was a strong feeling of heightened hearing that made everything seem more intense. I heard everything that exists both in the sky and on the ground. Although I experienced many frightening noises in hell, each sound lingered in my mind, creating a feeling of deep unease. How can I be insane, then, if I can clearly understand my thoughts and feelings? Hearken! Observe how calmly and healthily I can share the entire story with you. It is hard to know how the idea first came to me; however, once it was in my mind, it disturbed me day and night. There was no object present, which made the situation even more mysterious and confusing for everyone involved. There was no passion at all, although people expected it to be strong and vibrant in that moment. I loved the old man because he told stories that made me feel happy and wise. He had always treated me well, although I sometimes doubted his intentions in difficult situations. He had never said anything hurtful to me, although I sometimes wondered why he was always so kind. Although he offered me gold, I felt no desire for it at all. I believe it was his eye that caught my attention and made me feel uneasy about him. Yes, it was indeed this item that I was searching for, and it brought me great joy upon discovering it. He had a vulture's eye that looked pale blue, with a strange film covering it. Whenever it happened to me, I felt very scared. Therefore, I slowly decided to end the old man's life and free myself from his eye forever. Now, this is the main idea, and it is important to understand it clearly for our discussion. You think I am crazy, although I feel that my thoughts are clear and focused on what I believe. Crazy people often seem confused, although they might understand things in their own way, which makes communication challenging. However, you really needed to see how I was feeling at that moment. You would have admired how carefully I acted. I was cautious and thoughtful, hiding my true intentions as I began my work. I was always very kind to the old man, especially during the week before I took his life. Every night, around midnight, I carefully turned the latch on his door and opened it very gently. When I made a hole big enough for my head, I carefully placed a dark lantern inside, so no light could escape. Oh, you would have laughed to see how cleverly I managed to put it in place. I moved it
 slowly—very, very slowly, so that I might not disturb the old
 man’s sleep. It took me an hour to place my whole head within the
 opening so far that I could see him as he lay upon his bed. Ha! "Could a crazy person really be so clever as this?" As my mind became clear in the room, I carefully opened the lantern, knowing the hinges might make noise. I opened it just enough so a thin ray of light shone on the vulture-like eye. I did this for seven long nights, each time at midnight; however, the eye was always closed, making it impossible for me to finish my work. The old man did not bother me, but rather his Evil Eye caused my distress. Every morning, when the sun rose, I confidently entered the room. I spoke to him with courage, calling his name in a friendly way and asking how he had slept. You can see he would have been a very wise old man to guess that I quietly watched him while he slept every night at midnight. On the eighth night, I felt particularly careful as I slowly opened the door. A watch’s minute hand moves more quickly than did mine. Never before that night had I felt the extent of my own powers—of
 my sagacity. I could hardly hold back my strong feelings of happiness and success after achieving what I wanted. To think that I was there, slowly opening the door, while he had no idea about my hidden actions or thoughts. I chuckled quietly at the idea. However, he seemed to hear me because he suddenly moved, as if surprised. You might believe that I stepped away from the situation; however, that is not the case at all. His room was completely dark because the shutters were tightly closed, as he feared robbers. Therefore, I knew he could not see the door, so I kept pushing it open slowly. I had my head in, and was about to open the lantern, when my
 thumb slipped upon the tin fastening, and the old man sprang up
 in bed, crying out—“Who’s there? ”

 I kept quite still and said nothing. I stayed completely still for one hour; however, I did not hear him lie down during that time. He was still sitting up in bed, listening to the sounds. Although I did the same thing night after night, I wondered about the strange noises in the wall. At that moment, I heard a quiet groan, and I understood it came from deep fear. It was not a sound of pain or sadness; rather, it was something entirely different and surprising. It was a quiet sound that came from deep within the soul when filled with great wonder. I recognized the sound clearly, although it brought back many memories that were both comforting and unsettling. Many nights, exactly at midnight, when everyone else was asleep, my own fears rose up. Their dreadful echo deepened the frightening thoughts that troubled me. I say that I was very familiar with it, although others might not understand as well as I do. I understood the feelings of the old man and felt sorry for him, although I secretly found it amusing. I understood that he had been lying awake ever since he heard the first small noise while turning in bed. His fears had been steadily growing inside him for a long time, therefore he often felt anxious and uneasy about the future. He had been trying to imagine them without any reason; however, he could not make himself do it. He had been saying to himself—“It is nothing but the wind in
 the chimney—it is only a mouse crossing the floor,” or “It is
 merely a cricket which has made a single chirp. ” Yes, he had been
 trying to comfort himself with these suppositions: but he had
 found all in vain. All his efforts were pointless, because Death had approached him quietly, following with a dark shadow that surrounded the helpless victim. It was the sad feeling from the unseen shadow that made him sense my presence in the room, although he did not see or hear me. After waiting a long time, very patiently, without hearing him move, I decided to open a small crack in the lantern. So I carefully opened it, and you cannot imagine how quietly I did this. Finally, a faint light, like a spider's thread, came from the crack and shone directly on the vulture's eye. It was completely open, and I became very angry as I looked at it. I saw it with perfect distinctness—all a dull blue, with a
 hideous veil over it that chilled the very marrow in my bones;
 but I could see nothing else of the old man’s face or person: for
 I had directed the ray as if by instinct, precisely upon the
 damned spot. And have I not told you that what you mistake for madness is but
 over-acuteness of the sense? Now, I heard a soft and quick sound that felt like a watch ticking while wrapped in cotton. I recognized that sound, even though it brought back many memories that I could not forget. It was the beating of the
 old man’s heart. It increased my fury, as the beating of a drum
 stimulates the soldier into courage. However, I still held back and remained quiet, even though I wanted to speak out. I hardly took a breath, feeling a heavy weight on my chest because of my anxiety and fear. I held the lantern very still, although the night was dark and filled with strange sounds all around me. I tried to see how steady I could keep the light on the eye. Meanwhile, the terrible beating of his heart grew louder and more intense, creating a feeling of deep anxiety in him. It became faster and louder every moment, filling the space with an intense energy that was hard to ignore. The old man’s terror must have been
 extreme! It grew louder, I say, louder every moment! "Do you understand me clearly, or do you have doubts about my message?" I have told you that I feel nervous; therefore, I truly am nervous. At the dead hour of the night, the dreadful silence of the old house filled me with uncontrollable terror because of such a strange noise. However, I stayed still for a few more minutes, holding back my movements and thoughts as I waited. However, the beating became even louder and more intense, creating an overwhelming sense of fear in the room. I believed my heart might explode with emotion, although I tried to stay calm in that moment. A new wave of anxiety took hold of me, as I worried that the noise would disturb a neighbor. The old man’s hour had come! With a loud yell, I
 threw open the lantern and leaped into the room. He let out a loud scream, but it was just that one time. In a sudden move, I pulled him down to the floor, and then I pushed the heavy bed over him. I then smiled happily because I saw that the work was mostly finished. However, for many minutes, the heart continued to beat, making a soft sound that echoed in the silence. This did not bother me, however, because it would not be heard through the wall. Finally, it came to an end, leaving a sense of quiet and relief in the room. The old man had passed away, leaving behind memories and stories that touched the hearts of many people around him. I took away the bed to look closely at the body. Yes, he was completely lifeless, showing no signs of life or activity at all. I placed my hand on my heart and kept it there for several minutes, feeling its steady beat. The silence around him was thick and unsettling, for there was not a single heartbeat to be heard. He was completely lifeless, therefore there was no chance he could ever come back to life. His eye would no longer cause me any worry or pain. If you still believe I am crazy, you will change your mind when I explain the careful steps I took to hide the body. The night was passing, and I worked quickly. However, I did so without making any noise. First, I carefully took apart the body, which was a very difficult and unsettling task to do. I removed the head, arms, and legs from the body, which was necessary for my task and made me feel uneasy. I then picked up three wooden boards from the floor of the room and placed them between the small beams. I replaced the boards so skillfully that no one, not even him, could notice anything wrong. There was nothing to clean away; there were no stains or any spots of blood at all. I had been very cautious because I wanted to avoid any problems or unexpected situations that could disturb me. A tub had collected everything, which was surprising and amusing at the same time. ha! When I had made an end of these labors, it was four o’clock—still
 dark as midnight. As the bell sounded the hour, there came a
 knocking at the street door. I went downstairs to open it with a light heart, because I had nothing to fear anymore. Three men entered the room. They introduced themselves as police officers, speaking very smoothly and confidently, making it clear they were in charge. A neighbor heard a loud scream during the night. Therefore, they suspected something bad had happened. Meanwhile, information was given to the police. The officers were sent to search the place. I smiled, although I wondered what I truly had to fear in that moment. I welcomed the gentlemen to my home, hoping they would feel comfortable and enjoy their time with me. I said the loud scream came from me in a dream, although it felt very real and frightening. The old man I talked about was not in the country at that time. I showed my visitors around the house, however, they seemed very interested in every room we entered. I told them to look carefully, although I knew it might be difficult for them to find what they needed. I guided them carefully to his room, although some were hesitant about entering such a private space. I presented his treasures to them, and they were safe and untouched, shining brightly in the light. In my excitement, I brought chairs into the room, hoping they could rest from their tiredness. Meanwhile, I boldly sat on the very spot where the victim lay. The officers felt pleased with the results of their work, although they knew there were still challenges ahead. My behavior made them believe in me, even though I often felt uncertain about myself. I felt completely relaxed and comfortable in that moment, although I knew there were challenges ahead. They sat together, and although I spoke happily, they talked about familiar topics and shared stories. However, soon I noticed my face becoming pale, and I wished for them to leave me alone. My head hurt, and I imagined a ringing in my ears. However, they kept sitting and talking without stopping. The ringing became clearer, so I spoke more openly to escape the discomfort. However, the sound persisted and intensified until I realized it was not in my ears. There is no doubt that I became very pale, but I spoke more smoothly and with a louder voice. However, the sound became louder, and I felt helpless because I didn't know what to do. It was a soft and muffled sound, like the ticking of a watch wrapped in cotton. I gasped for breath, however the officers did not hear me at all. I spoke more quickly and with more passion; however, the noise kept getting louder and louder. I got up and argued about small matters, speaking loudly and using big gestures. However, the noise around me kept getting louder. "Why are they still here?" I walked back and forth with strong steps, feeling angry because of what the men said. However, the noise kept getting louder. Oh God! What options do I have to choose from? I was very angry and shouted loudly. I expressed my feelings with strong words, although it didn't help my situation. I moved the chair I had been sitting on and dragged it across the floor. However, the noise grew louder and seemed endless. The sound became so intense that it seemed to grow stronger and stronger, creating a feeling of unease. Meanwhile, the men continued to chat happily and exchanged smiles, creating a warm and friendly atmosphere. Was it possible that they did not hear anything at all? Oh, powerful God! —no, no! They heard something unusual in the distance, causing them to feel worried and curious about what it could be. They had their doubts, however, they could not find enough proof to be completely sure. They understood everything, although they did not openly show their awareness of the situation around them. They were making fun of my fear, which made me feel even more alone and upset. I reflected on this idea, and I still believe it holds true today. However, anything would be better than feeling this deep pain and suffering that filled my heart. Anything felt better than being laughed at like this, although I could not think of what that might be. I could no longer stand those fake smiles people gave me. They felt insincere, and it hurt me deeply. I felt a strong urge to scream because the situation was unbearable and I could hardly hold back my feelings. And now, this situation has happened again, causing me to feel a mix of frustration and disbelief. —hark! louder! louder! louder! _louder! _

 “Villains! ” I shrieked, “dissemble no more! I accept my actions. I know what I did was wrong, and I feel regret for my choices. "Although it is hard, we must remove the boards carefully to make things better." "Although it may seem confusing, the repeated phrase 'here, here!' suggests a strong desire for attention or agreement." It is the loud pounding of his ugly heart that he cannot ignore.
